tldr: Segmentation-based hybrid model using a frozen DINOv2 encoder and a lightweight CNN decoder to detect and localize image manipulations, evaluated with F1-score on a holdout split.
summary: This notebook implements a segmentation-driven pipeline for scientific image forgery detection. It combines a frozen self-supervised DINOv2 visual encoder with a lightweight custom CNN decoder to produce pixel-level manipulation masks. The model is trained for 4 epochs using BCEWithLogitsLoss and AdamW on an 80/20 holdout split. Inference includes gradient-enhanced adaptive thresholding and morphological refinement, followed by a dual-condition classification rule based on mask area and mean probability. The author evaluates segmentation performance using F1-score and generates RLE-encoded submissions.
extensive_summary:
## Architecture & Setup
The solution uses a hybrid segmentation model combining a frozen DINOv2 base encoder with a lightweight CNN decoder (`DinoTinyDecoder`). The encoder extracts semantic features, which are reshaped into a feature map and passed through three convolutional layers (768→256→64→1) with ReLU activations to produce a binary probability map. All images and masks are resized to 512×512.

## Training Pipeline
Authentic images are paired with zero masks, while forged images use provided `.npy` binary masks. Data is split 80/20 via `train_test_split` with `random_state=42`. The model is trained for 4 epochs with batch size 1, learning rate 1e-5, and weight decay 2e-5 using `AdamW`. Only the CNN decoder head is trainable; DINOv2 remains frozen. Loss is `BCEWithLogitsLoss`.

## Inference & Post-Processing
During inference, the model outputs a probability map. This is refined using gradient enhancement (Sobel gradients normalized and blended with alpha=0.35) followed by Gaussian blur. An adaptive threshold is applied (`mean + 0.3 * std`). Morphological closing (5×5) and opening (3×3) clean the mask. Final classification uses a dual rule: if `mask_area < 400` or `mean_probability_inside_mask < 0.35`, the image is labeled authentic; otherwise, forged. Masks are resized to original dimensions and RLE-encoded for submission.

## Evaluation
Segmentation quality is measured via F1-score on a validation subset of forged images, comparing predicted masks against ground truth. Visualization cells demonstrate mask alignment on both forged and authentic samples.
post_processing: gradient-enhanced adaptive thresholding (mean + 0.3*std), Sobel gradient blending, Gaussian blur, morphological close/open, area/mean probability filtering for classification, RLE encoding
notable_techniques: Frozen DINOv2 encoder with lightweight CNN decoder for segmentation
Gradient-enhanced adaptive thresholding for mask refinement
Dual-condition classification rule (area < 400 or mean prob < 0.35) to filter false positives
